## LLM Dimensions (60% of score)

| Dimension | Wt | v2 | v1 | General |
|-----------|:--:|:--:|:--:|:-------:|
| Mandate Relevance            | 20% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Data Grounding & Source Citation | 16% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 3/5 |
| Quantitative Accuracy & Calculation Discipline | 16% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 1/5 |
| Output Structure Completeness | 12% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 1/5 |
| Action Specificity           | 12% | 5/5 | 3/5 | 1/5 |
| Data Integrity & Gap Disclosure | 12% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Trend & Continuity           | 12% | 5/5 | 2/5 | 2/5 |


### Reasoning

**v2 Agent:** The v2 brief demonstrates the highest quality across all dimensions: every sector row carries a named direction, urgency, and quantified impact traceable to a named source; all four required calculations (EIBOR sensitivity with ±25/50bps scenarios, oil revenue in USD and AED with explicit FX conversion, Operation 300bn gap-closed percentage using the correct baseline method) are present and methodologically disclosed; streak language (5th consecutive Fed hold, 214 consecutive CBUAE unchanged days, 4th consecutive rising Brent session) is drawn from cross-session state.json, satisfying the Trend & Continuity dimension fully; and action flags name specific EDB teams, concrete trigger thresholds, and reassessment milestones.

**v1 Agent:** The v1 brief is well-structured and EDB-mandate-aligned, with a clean sector matrix, a prominent AED key number (AED 1,448mn feedstock impact), and honest disclosure of data staleness and tool failures including the OPEC basket null return and the 3.2 mb/d vs 3.8 mb/d production discrepancy; however, action flags lack the specificity of named EDB teams and concrete trigger thresholds, and the brief is structurally capped at 2/5 for Trend & Continuity because it has no cross-session state.json access and therefore cannot produce verified streak counts or prior-run baselines.

**General Agent:** The general brief reads as a competent narrative financial summary but fails almost every EDB-specific scoring dimension: there is no sector impact matrix, no EIBOR sensitivity calculation block, no oil revenue figure in AED, no Operation 300bn analysis, no action flags with named teams or trigger conditions, and no disclosure of estimation methods or data vintage; source citations are present but inconsistently linked to specific numbers, and the brief is also structurally capped at 2/5 for Trend & Continuity given the absence of state.json access, with no streak language of any kind appearing in the text.
